an9.36:1.1: "I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the first *jhāna*, there is the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an9.36:1.2: I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the second *jhāna*, there is the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an9.36:1.3: I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the third *jhāna*, there is the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an9.36:1.4: I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the fourth *jhāna*, there is the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an9.36:1.5: I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the dimension [*āyatana*] of infinite space, there is the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an9.36:1.6: I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the dimension [*āyatana*] of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇa*], there is the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an9.36:1.7: I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the dimension [*āyatana*] of nothingness, there is the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an9.36:1.8: I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the dimension [*āyatana*] of neither-*saññā*-nor-non-*saññā*, there is the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an9.36:1.9: I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the cessation of *saññā* and feeling, there is the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an9.36:10.1: Thus, *bhikkhus*, as far as there are attainments with *saññā*, so far there is penetration of final knowledge.
an9.36:10.3: the attainment of the dimension [*āyatana*] of neither-*saññā*-nor-non-*saññā* and the cessation of *saññā* and feeling—these, *bhikkhus*, should be rightly declared by meditators [*jhāyī*] who are skilled in attainments and skilled in emerging from attainments, after they have entered and emerged from them, I say."
an9.36:2.1: 'I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the first *jhāna*, there is the destruction of the *āsavas*'—this is what was said.
an9.36:2.2: And with reference to what was this said?
an9.36:2.3: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, secluded [*viveka*] from sensual pleasures … enters and dwells in the first *jhāna*.
an9.36:2.4: Whatever there is of form [*rūpa*], *vedanā*, *saññā*, *saṅkhāras*, consciousness [*viññāṇa*], he contemplates those *dhammas* as impermanent [*anicca*], as *dukkha*, as a disease, as a boil, as a dart, as misery, as an affliction, as alien, as disintegrating, as empty, as not-self [*anattā*].
an9.36:2.5: He turns his mind [*citta*] away from those *dhammas*.
an9.36:2.6: Having turned his mind [*citta*] away from those *dhammas*, he directs his mind [*citta*] to the deathless element:
an9.36:2.7: 'This is peaceful, this is sublime, that is, the stilling of all *saṅkhāras*, the relinquishing of all attachments, the destruction of *taṇhā*, dispassion, cessation, *nibbāna*.'
an9.36:2.8: Established there, he attains the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an9.36:2.9: If he does not attain the destruction of the *āsavas*, then through that very desire for the *dhamma* and delight in the *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he is spontaneously reborn and attains final *nibbāna* there, not returning from that world.
an9.36:3.1: Just as, *bhikkhus*, an archer or an archer's apprentice, having practiced on a grass dummy or a clay mound, later becomes a far-shooter, able to pierce a hair, and able to split a large body;
an9.36:3.10: it is with reference to this that this was said.
an9.36:3.2: in the same way, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, secluded [*viveka*] from sensual pleasures … enters and dwells in the first *jhāna*.
an9.36:3.3: Whatever there is of form [*rūpa*], *vedanā*, *saññā*, *saṅkhāras*, consciousness [*viññāṇa*], he contemplates those *dhammas* as impermanent [*anicca*], as *dukkha*, as a disease, as a boil, as a dart, as misery, as an affliction, as alien, as disintegrating, as empty, as not-self [*anattā*].
an9.36:3.4: He turns his mind [*citta*] away from those *dhammas*.
an9.36:3.5: Having turned his mind [*citta*] away from those *dhammas*, he directs his mind [*citta*] to the deathless element:
an9.36:3.6: 'This is peaceful, this is sublime, that is, the stilling of all *saṅkhāras*, the relinquishing of all attachments, the destruction of *taṇhā*, dispassion, cessation, *nibbāna*.'
an9.36:3.7: Established there, he attains the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an9.36:3.8: If he does not attain the destruction of the *āsavas*, then through that very desire for the *dhamma* and delight in the *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he is spontaneously reborn and attains final *nibbāna* there, not returning from that world.
an9.36:3.9: 'I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the first *jhāna*, there is the destruction of the *āsavas*'—
an9.36:4.1: I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the second *jhāna* …
an9.36:4.10: Established there, he attains the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an9.36:4.11: If he does not attain the destruction of the *āsavas*, then through that very desire for the *dhamma* and delight in the *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he is spontaneously reborn and attains final *nibbāna* there, not returning from that world.
an9.36:4.2: I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the third *jhāna* …
an9.36:4.3: 'I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the fourth *jhāna*, there is the destruction of the *āsavas*'—this is what was said.
an9.36:4.4: And with reference to what was this said?
an9.36:4.5: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, with the abandoning of *sukha* and *dukkha*, and with the earlier disappearance of joy and displeasure, enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*, which is neither painful nor pleasant, with purity of *sati* and equanimity [*upekkhā*].
an9.36:4.6: Whatever there is of form [*rūpa*], *vedanā*, *saññā*, *saṅkhāras*, consciousness [*viññāṇa*], he contemplates those *dhammas* as impermanent [*anicca*], as *dukkha*, as a disease, as a boil, as a dart, as misery, as an affliction, as alien, as disintegrating, as empty, as not-self [*anattā*].
an9.36:4.7: He turns his mind [*citta*] away from those *dhammas*.
an9.36:4.8: Having turned his mind [*citta*] away from those *dhammas*, he directs his mind [*citta*] to the deathless element:
an9.36:4.9: 'This is peaceful, this is sublime, that is, the stilling of all *saṅkhāras*, the relinquishing of all attachments, the destruction of *taṇhā*, dispassion, cessation, *nibbāna*.'
an9.36:5.1: Just as, *bhikkhus*, an archer or an archer's apprentice, having practiced on a grass dummy or a clay mound, later becomes a far-shooter, able to pierce a hair, and able to split a large body;
an9.36:5.2: in the same way, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, with the abandoning of *sukha* … enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*.
an9.36:5.5: 'I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the fourth *jhāna*, there is the destruction of the *āsavas*'—it is with reference to this that this was said.
an9.36:6.1: 'I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the dimension [*āyatana*] of infinite space, there is the destruction of the *āsavas*'—this is what was said.
an9.36:6.2: And with reference to what was this said?
an9.36:6.3: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, with the complete transcending of perceptions of form [*rūpa*], with the disappearance of perceptions of resistance, with non-attention to perceptions of diversity, perceiving 'infinite space,' enters and dwells in the dimension [*āyatana*] of infinite space.
an9.36:6.4: Whatever there is of *vedanā*, *saññā*, *saṅkhāras*, consciousness [*viññāṇa*], he contemplates those *dhammas* as impermanent [*anicca*], as *dukkha*, as a disease, as a boil, as a dart, as misery, as an affliction, as alien, as disintegrating, as empty, as not-self [*anattā*].
an9.36:6.5: He turns his mind [*citta*] away from those *dhammas*.
an9.36:6.6: Having turned his mind [*citta*] away from those *dhammas*, he directs his mind [*citta*] to the deathless element:
an9.36:6.7: 'This is peaceful, this is sublime, that is, the stilling of all *saṅkhāras*, the relinquishing of all attachments, the destruction of *taṇhā*, dispassion, cessation, *nibbāna*.'
an9.36:6.8: Established there, he attains the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an9.36:6.9: If he does not attain the destruction of the *āsavas*, then through that very desire for the *dhamma* and delight in the *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he is spontaneously reborn and attains final *nibbāna* there, not returning from that world.
an9.36:7.1: Just as, *bhikkhus*, an archer or an archer's apprentice, having practiced on a grass dummy or a clay mound, later becomes a far-shooter, able to pierce a hair, and able to split a large body;
an9.36:7.2: in the same way, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, with the complete transcending of perceptions of form [*rūpa*], with the disappearance of perceptions of resistance, with non-attention to perceptions of diversity, perceiving 'infinite space,' enters and dwells in the dimension [*āyatana*] of infinite space.
an9.36:7.5: 'I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the dimension [*āyatana*] of infinite space, there is the destruction of the *āsavas*'—
an9.36:7.6: it is with reference to this that this was said.
an9.36:8.1: 'I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the dimension [*āyatana*] of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇa*] …
an9.36:8.10: If he does not attain the destruction of the *āsavas*, then through that very desire for the *dhamma* and delight in the *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he is spontaneously reborn and attains final *nibbāna* there, not returning from that world.
an9.36:8.2: I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the dimension [*āyatana*] of nothingness, there is the destruction of the *āsavas*'—this is what was said.
an9.36:8.3: And with reference to what was this said?
an9.36:8.4: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, completely transcending the dimension [*āyatana*] of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇa*], perceiving 'there is nothing,' enters and dwells in the dimension [*āyatana*] of nothingness.
an9.36:8.5: Whatever there is of *vedanā*, *saññā*, *saṅkhāras*, consciousness [*viññāṇa*], he contemplates those *dhammas* as impermanent [*anicca*], as *dukkha*, as a disease, as a boil, as a dart, as misery, as an affliction, as alien, as disintegrating, as empty, as not-self [*anattā*].
an9.36:8.6: He turns his mind [*citta*] away from those *dhammas*.
an9.36:8.7: Having turned his mind [*citta*] away from those *dhammas*, he directs his mind [*citta*] to the deathless element:
an9.36:8.8: 'This is peaceful, this is sublime, that is, the stilling of all *saṅkhāras*, the relinquishing of all attachments, the destruction of *taṇhā*, dispassion, cessation, *nibbāna*.'
an9.36:8.9: Established there, he attains the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an9.36:9.1: Just as, *bhikkhus*, an archer or an archer's apprentice, having practiced on a grass dummy or a clay mound, later becomes a far-shooter, able to pierce a hair, and able to split a large body;
an9.36:9.10: it is with reference to this that this was said.
an9.36:9.2: in the same way, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, completely transcending the dimension [*āyatana*] of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇa*], perceiving 'there is nothing,' enters and dwells in the dimension [*āyatana*] of nothingness.
an9.36:9.3: Whatever there is of *vedanā*, *saññā*, *saṅkhāras*, consciousness [*viññāṇa*], he contemplates those *dhammas* as impermanent [*anicca*], as *dukkha*, as a disease, as a boil, as a dart, as misery, as an affliction, as alien, as disintegrating, as empty, as not-self [*anattā*].
an9.36:9.4: He turns his mind [*citta*] away from those *dhammas*.
an9.36:9.5: Having turned his mind [*citta*] away from those *dhammas*, he directs his mind [*citta*] to the deathless element:
an9.36:9.6: 'This is peaceful, this is sublime, that is, the stilling of all *saṅkhāras*, the relinquishing of all attachments, the destruction of *taṇhā*, dispassion, cessation, *nibbāna*.'
an9.36:9.7: Established there, he attains the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an9.36:9.8: If he does not attain the destruction of the *āsavas*, then through that very desire for the *dhamma* and delight in the *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he is spontaneously reborn and attains final *nibbāna* there, not returning from that world.
an9.36:9.9: 'I say, *bhikkhus*, that depending on the dimension [*āyatana*] of nothingness, there is the destruction of the *āsavas*'—
